The non-marker rows' blocks are at cols 23 and 35, which are the same columns as some of the marker rows' blocks. This suggests that the non-marker rows' colors are determined by the marker rows' colors at cols 23 and 35.
But I've established that only the top red marker affects the non-marker rows. So the non-marker rows' colors are determined by row 17's colors at cols 23 and 35.

But row 17 has 5 blocks and rows 26 and 35 have 10 blocks, so they can't match directly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the yellow dots to see if they change
# Yellow dots are at: (25, 34), (25, 37), (28, 34), (28, 37), (34, 34), (34, 37), (37, 34), (37, 37)

# First, let me check the current state of the yellow dots
seg = current_frame.segmentation
yellow_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Yellow dots: {len(yellow_dots)}")
for dot in yellow_dots:
    boundary = dot['boundary']
    row = boundary[0][0]
    col = boundary[0][1]
    print(f"  ({row}, {col})")

# Try clicking one yellow dot
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 34}])
print(f"\nAfter clicking yellow dot at (25, 34):")
print(f"  Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if the yellow dot changed
seg_after = current_frame.segmentation
yellow_dots_after = [n for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Yellow dots after: {len(yellow_dots_after)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
